title: 3. Data Center Cooling Market Analysis
summary: AI workloads and high-performance computing: The surge in AI, cloud, and HPC workloads is increasing thermal loads and rack power densities,
data_points: AI workloads and high-performance computing: The surge in AI, cloud, and HPC workloads is increasing thermal loads and rack power densities, necessitating advanced cooling solut...
Rising rack power density: Medium-density racks (5–9 kW) currently dominate, but high-density racks (>9 kW) are growing rapidly, especially in hyperscale and AI environments 17.

Energy efficiency regulations: In regions such as Europe, stringent energy and carbon regulations are accelerating the shift toward green cooling technologies 19.

title: 15. Appendix: Technical Specifications
summary: Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs): Scalable from 105 kW to 2.5 MW, designed in collaboration with leading silicon manufacturers and certifie
data_points: Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs): Scalable from 105 kW to 2.5 MW, designed in collaboration with leading silicon manufacturers and certified for NVIDIA hardware, enabling megaw...
ChilledDoor rear‑door heat exchangers: Rack‑agnostic units capable of cooling up to 75 kW per rack, ideal for GPU‑intensive workloads 40.
Liquid‑to‑Air Heat Dissipation Units (HDU™): Compact units (600 mm width) delivering 100 kW of heat rejection, or up to 132 kW when creating a water loop, matching NVIDIA NVL144...
Floor‑standing (liquid‑to‑liquid) CDUs: Isolate facility water from the IT loop, optimized for high‑density AI workloads 69.

title: Conclusion & Key Takeaways
